text: 4. Food Adulteration Food adulteration has become a serious problem in recent years, especially in developing countries like ours. It refers to the practice of mixing or adding harmful, inferior, or toxic substances to food items in order to increase profit. Unscrupulous traders and dishonest businessmen use this method to cheat consumers and maximize their earnings, but in doing so, they endanger human health and society as a whole. Common examples of adulteration include mixing water with milk, adding soap or chemicals to sweets for better color and texture, using formalin to preserve fish and fruits, mixing brick dust with chili powder, and applying artificial colors to vegetables and confectionery items. These harmful chemicals and substances not only reduce the nutritional value of food but also cause severe diseases like diarrhea, kidney failure, cancer, high blood pressure, and even death. The situation worsens because consumers are often unaware of such adulteration and purchase these foods unknowingly. Adulterated food also weakens the next generation by damaging children’s growth and immunity. To fight against this evil practice, the government must enforce strict laws and punish those who engage in food adulteration. Modern testing facilities should be made available to detect unsafe foods in markets. At the same time, people must remain alert and conscious while buying food items. Public awareness campaigns, social responsibility, and ethical business practices can play a vital role in eradicating this problem. If we fail to address the issue immediately, food adulteration will create a health catastrophe that threatens the progress of our nation. Therefore, it is our moral duty to resist this menace and ensure safe, pure, and nutritious food for all.

text: 5. Deforestation Deforestation is the process of cutting down of trees at a large scale or indiscriminately. The word deforestation is the opposite to afforestation. Deforestation poses a serious threat to the existence of all the living beings mankind, animals and plants. Tress keep the ecological balance in the environment. To keep an ecological balance in the nature 4 portion of the total land area of the earth should have forest. Human beings inhale oxygen and exhale carbon oxide in breathing. On the other hand, the trees take carbon dioxide and produces oxygen in the atmosphere which keeps a balance for living. But due to deforestation the earth is going to be a threatened, dangerous and a risky zone for human habitat. For deforestation or for the lack of afforestation, different sorts of natural calamities like global warming, climate changes, drought or heavy downpour, prolong flood or water logging, the green house effect have been escalated to a great extent. So, to save our mother earth for us and for the future generation, in no time we have to plant saplings, seedlings and Trees more and more at any suitable piece of land. To save us from the grip of deforestation, we have to intensify and accelerate, afforestation program --a revolutionary change must be brought in every place across the whole world. To impart education, the adverse effect of deforestation must be the integral part of our academic curriculum. text: 6. Global Warming Global Warming is a matter of serious concern for our mother earth. Climate change is fully responsible for it. And behind this degradation of the nature, humans and their machines are mostly liable. In between recent few decades, the temperature is climbing alarmingly and faster than any time of the past. Due to global warming, the ice caps of the South and North poles are melting fast and many countries are already experiencing adverse impacts of it. Among the other adverse impacts, irregular monsoon,heavy rainfall causing prolong water logging, landslide and as well as salinity of water as well as river erosion, drought, cold spell, and so on. Besides, as the globe warms up, the weather gets more violent and extreme and causes frequent storm and cyclone. If it continues, at a stage, many parts of our beloved earth belonging to some of the best land for growing food will be overflooded and the flood water will swallow the huge area of human habitat. Even some big cities like, London, kolkata, Bangkok and many other big cities will be submerged. In a word, if the global warming mounts such rapidly the mother earth will lose its ecological balance and human habitat will be threatened i.e, the existence of all living being will be at high risk and in great danger. To address this burning issue and to keep our next generation safe and sound, we all have to use environment -friendly machineries and to plant more and more tress to keep the plannet green and carbon dioxide free.

text: 7. A street accident The invention of a motorized vehicle is both a boon and blessing of modern science but sometimes it's a curse when an accident happens and brings severe casualties' to human lives. However, nothing is without unmixed blessings in this world. In fact, the road communication in modern era has added a new dimension to the human lives and civilization but the means of this fast communication, streets are not free from accidents. Usually it happens in the highways where motorized vehicles run at a great speed in fast driving lanes and routes, or in the busy turning points, or in the zigzag road. There are many causes behind a street accident. The most important reasons of them are: defective vehicles and roads, lack of awareness of the drivers, long time on long drive without proper rest, lack of sufficient training, very fast speed of the vehicles, zigzag road, pot holes, lack of underpasses or foot over bridges, unawareness of the traffic rules, lack of traffic lights at the important crossing points, lack of zebra crossing for the passers-by, unlicensed drivers etc, However, a street accident may not be only a very trifle matter or the talking topic. An accident not only is a big loss, pains and agony of the near and dear ones to the bereaved family, but it is surely very painful, irreparable loss and great shock to the society or nation in different respects. So, to address this grave issue, the concerned authority and the conscious citizen of the country have to take collaborative measures and action to uproot the causes of the street accidents.

text: 8. Traffic jam Traffic means vehicles and jam stands for congestion or blocking or clogging. So, Traffic jam is a long line of vehicles on a road that cannot move or that can only move very slowly. It is the common phenomenon in busy and mega cities. There are so many reasons behind traffic jam. Mainly, narrower roads, defective and slow speedy vehicles like rickshaws and push-carts or motorless vehicles, unpermitted road crossing by passers-by and parking and turning, flaws in traffic rules and management system or mismanagement of the authority, loading and unloading goods and passengers here and there, are responsible for traffic jam. Besides, poor condition of the roads, flooding, unplanned and uncoordinated digging of the different authorities like RHD, WASA,TNT and Electric Supply Authority also cause traffic jam a lot. On working days mostly in pick hours roads are packed with the traffic jam and precious time is killed or lost in vain. Due to traffic jam office going people are late. Students can't attend classes or take part in exams timely. Ambulances carrying dying/serious patients can't reach hospitals in time for better or follow up treatment and fire brigades can't reach the spot to extinguish the fire in time. Even, it happens sometimes that patients meet their immature deaths on the way. To eliminate traffic jam, spacious roads, more fly-overs, well planned Udner-Passes and Foot-Over-Bridges should be built. On the other hand, speed- wise seperate lanes, strictness in traffic rules, modernisation of traffic systems can address the issue of traffic jam to this end.

text: 9. A Rickshaw puller person who pulls a rickshaw in order to earn his living is called a rickshaw puller. He is a very familiar figure across the country. In rural areas he is a shithole dweller or lives in a shabby hut or in the towns or the cities he lives in a slum. Usually his dress is torn and has no rickshaw of his own. He has to pull his rickshaw in both fine and rough weather to earn a square meal. Generally, a rickshaw puller living in a mega city earns more than his counterpart living in the rural areas. As rickshaw fare has no hard and fast rule. So, the fare is settled between the rickshaw puller and the passenger instantly by mutual negotiation. Nowadays rickshaw fare is very rampant. It's totally beyond the means of low-earning common mass. However, a rickshaw puller's life is full of sorrows and sufferings. When he suffers from a malady or an illness he cannot pull his rickshaw and cannot earn money for living. On the consequence, he and his family have to starve. But the day he earns more his face beams with joy. Really, his hope and expectation is not so lofty or sky scrapping or sky touching. He is contended whatever he earns. Though he cannot eat very well with his scanty income he can sleep very well. To conclude, a rickshaw puller keeps an important role in the communication of short distance and for the low and middle income people. In Bangladesh, on strike or hartal days a rickshaw is the only means of transport to ease our communication.

text: 10. Our School Library A library is an abode of gaining knowledge and information. It is a place where a huge collection of books, newspapers. journals, magazines as well as CDs, DVDs for electronic device on different branches of knowledge are kept for learners to read, study, or for research work. Every renowned school has a library. so, do we. There are two sections in our library: one is borrowing section and another is reference section. One student can use these resource items in the library or one can borrow these according to his/her need. But one cannot borrow anything from the reference section. It satisfies our eternal thirst for learning. Indeed, a library is an integral part of a school. No school is thorough without a library. Ours is a very big modern library in the first floor of the main school building. It is fully equipped with all the modern facilities such as modern furniture, fully air conditioned, access to online etc. our school library runs under a trained up librarian and her two assistants. About 100 students can read here at a time. A huge collection of books amounting more than 10,000 copies are available in our school library. Every year some new and latest books are bought and procured from various sources so that a student can keep abreast of all the latest and update information. Here all the academic and reference books are arranged into classes or groups and then sorted alphabetically for the quick access to the particular or desired books from the prolific collection of books. Here, learners have to have membership cards for both reading and borrowing books. Books are issued with the library cards very promptly and easily with the assistance of the catalogue. Borrowing books are validated only for two weeks. Not more than 2 books can be issued against a membership at a time. For the smooth function of the library, sometimes students are penalized for the delay return of the borrowing books. Our library opens at 9 a.m. and closes at 4 p.m except holidays. The maxim' Silence is golden' is strictly in force in our library. I am really ecstatic and feel proud of having such an enriched library.

text: 11. Our National Flag Every independent nation of the world has a national flag. So, do we. A national flag is the pictogram or symbol of a country's independence, sovereignty and her identity of being an independent state. Our national flag is our pride and our entity on the world's map. Very few nations of the world had to fight and sacrifice their countrymen's invaluable lives and blood to achieve its independence and to get its national flag. Hence, we have a different story to get it. Our glorious national flag is purchased at a great price i.e. by dint of millions people's blood. Painter Quamrul Hasan is the designer of our national flag. It is rectangular in shape. It has two colors: red in the centre and green around the center. Its centre looks like a rising sun on the background of green field. It has different sizes for different purposes but it will be made in proportion to 10:6 i.e. if the length is 10 feet, the width is 6 feet or if the length is 10 inches, the width is 6 inches. Here, the rising sun or the red disc represents the supreme sacrifice and courage of our people of the country and the green color symbolizes the prosperity, greenery, vitality and youthfulness etc. As a whole, our national flag emblematizes the sovereignty and the unity of Bangladesh. The national flag is hoisted atop in the Government, semi-government, autonomous and non-government any other organizations but it is kept lowered or hoisted at half ma non-government en bassies or mast on national mourning day or any other mourning days celebrated nationally. Our heart swells up with joy when we see our national flugs flying. In a word, it is our pride, spirit, hope, inspiration and more. So, to uphold its value and significance, we all must keep our national flag at its sublime position at any cost at any moment.

text: 12. The life of a farmer A farmer is a person who lives on farming, i.e., grows crops to earn his living. He usually lives in the rural areas so that he can have easy access to wide open fields and plains where he can cultivate. He lives from hand to mouth. Не leads a rather miserable life by no means a luxurious one. He lives in a simple homestead-not so well furnished with modern amenities. He toils hard day and night in his fields to grow more and more food to feed the whole nation. He works from dawn to dust. He nurtures the tender plants like his own kids. He takes care of his crops as much as he can. When his crops grow in the fullest scale, he realizes his hard work has paid off. But his hard labour seldom pays off. Most often he is cheated from his rightful earnings. He is never respected but always downtrodden in our society. He is considered a worthless or illiterate. His profession is not recognised as a prestigious career in our society. Rather he is looked down upon and weighed down in the society. But a farmer is he who feeds us- the entire nation with his gracious labor. We cannot survive if the farmers stop farming. His valour and chivalrous labuor towards cultivation guarantee us the adequate food. Really he works for nourishing us; to keep the whole nation healthy, wealthy and free from starvation. His debt can never be repaid. We are indebted to him. So, a farmer is the golden son of any nation.

text: 13. A winter morning A winter morning may be cold and foggy but very pleasant and comfortable while basking in the sun and lounging under the quilt. Everything seems hazy and dim. The grass remains wet with dew drops. When the sun rises, dew drops sparkle like pearls and wither soon. Old people and children shiver in cold. Poor people gather straw and make fire to bask in the heat. Animals, too are helpless. They hide themselves in the corner of the houses and try to save themselves from the biting cold outside. And the wild animals stay at their respective cavers and the birds stay at their nests. People get up late in the morning. Old people and children get up a bit later to evade cold of the early morning. Working people get out of the doors when the fog disappears and when the sun is hot. In a winter morning, people usually eat homemade cakes and piayas with 'date juice' is the sun or sitting outside their homes to enjoy the warmth of the early sun. Bangladeshi migrants settled across the globe come to enjoy and take the taste of winter morning in Bangladesh. They find the winter morning here pleasant and enjoyable as the winter in any European country is tremendously bitter and cold. A winter morning in Bangladesh is thus quite fascinating for them. On the other hand, a winter morning is a little bit bane for the poor but a boon for the rich

text: 14. Climate Change Climate change is the most pressing/talked about issue at the moment over the world. The cause of climate change can be divided into two categories those are due to natural causes and those are created by men. Global worming is the main natural reason and using harmful chemicals in every day life the main reason created by man. The climate is changing rapidly that result in the rise of the earth's average temperature. The global climate change and its consequences are leaving a bad impact on the developing countries to face natural calamities and poverty. Climate change impacts include temperature rise, greenhouse and carbon dioxide gas emissions, erratic rainfall, salinity intrusion, rise of floods, cyclones, storm surges and draught, ice sheets melting which will seriously affect the agriculture and livelihood, especially at the poor. Bangladesh, for its geographical locations, is likely to be the most affected. The developed nations, which are more responsible for such climate changes, should take responsibilities to protect the victimized countries. The Maldives is also one of the worst victims of climate change. A one meter sea-level rise will submerge about one-third of the total area of Bangladesh, which will uproot 25-30 million people of Bangladesh. These people will become refuges of climate change. To reduce the bad impact of climate change people should be aware. Tree plantation can reduce global warming which is the main natural reason of climate change. Stop using harmful chemicals can reduce environmental pollution which is the main man made reason of climate change. Students, environmentalists and all other concerned should be careful to protect the environment and raise awareness and can play a vital role to reduce bad/adverse impact of climate change.

text: 15. A Rainy Day A rainy day is always special and full of beauty. When dark clouds gather in the sky and cool wind begins to blow, nature looks calm and peaceful. Suddenly, the rain starts falling in tiny drops and then turns into heavy showers. Gradually, the rain becomes heavier, and the sound of raindrops on tin roofs, trees, and roads creates a pleasant music. The whole environment turns cool and calm. Nature seems to smile under the refreshing touch of rain.On a rainy day, the roads become wet and sometimes muddy. Water gathers in low-lying areas, and people move carefully with umbrellas or raincoats. In cities, traffic jams often occur due to waterlogging. However, children feel very happy. They love to play in the rain, float paper boats in small streams, and jump into puddles of water. In villages, farmers welcome the rain because it is a blessing for their crops. The fields look greener and fresher after rainfall. The rain washes away dust and pollution, making everything look clean and bright. A rainy day also brings a special feeling of comfort. Many people prefer to stay at home, enjoy hot tea, and eat delicious snacks while watching the rain from their windows. Sometimes schools and offices remain closed due to heavy rainfall, which adds extra joy for students. However, poor people who live in fragile houses suffer a lot during continuous rain. Floods may damage crops and homes. In spite of some difficulties, a rainy day brings peace, freshness, and new life to nature. It refreshes our mind and fills our heart with joy. Truly, a rainy day is a beautiful gift of nature that adds charm to our everyday life.

text: 16. A tea stall A tea stall is a kind of shop where tea as well as biscuits, snacks, beetle leaves are sold. It is usually seen at bus stops, rail stations, Water Transport Terminal, inside the airports, Ferry Ghat, university or college campus, close reach of the offices or any suitable public places where mass people throng to have a cup of tea. Tea is served hot but in some exclusive and expensive restaurant ice tea is also served. Though a tea stall has no regular or binding time table. It generally opens early in the morning and closes at midnight. As a whole, all walks of people come to a tea stall except some reserved and VIPs. If it is a foggy winter morning, the poor and the common mass seem crazy to sip over the cup of hot tea as a warm up session of the day. Most of the time of the day, a tea stall is found busy and lively with the discussion of the recent key and touching topics of the politics or important social, national or global issues. And, sometimes, it becomes the mini- parliament of the mass people. Through the discussion mass people can be aware of their duties and responsibilities, right and wrong. They can build up awareness among themselves. Thus, a tea stall keeps a vital role to make the people aware of the growing concern of the economical, social or political related matters.

text: 17. A book fair A book fair is a wonderful event where publishers, booksellers, writers, and readers come together to celebrate the world of books. It is usually held in a large open space or exhibition hall and attracts people of all ages. The main purpose of a book fair is to promote reading habits and introduce people to new books, authors, and ideas. Stalls are set up by different publishers displaying books on various subjects such as literature, science, history, religion, art, and children’s stories. People can browse through the collections, read summaries, and buy books at reasonable prices. Many book fairs also organize special events like book signings, discussions, storytelling sessions, and cultural programs, which make the atmosphere lively and engaging. In Bangladesh, the most famous book fair is the Ekushey Boi Mela, held every February to honor the martyrs of the Language Movement. This fair not only sells books but also preserves our culture, language, and history. A book fair inspires people to read more and develop their knowledge. It is also a meeting place for authors and readers to share ideas and experiences. Visiting a book fair is both enjoyable and educational because it opens the door to the vast world of literature. It teaches us that books are our true friends, guiding us toward wisdom, imagination, and enlightenment. In today’s digital age, a book fair reminds us of the irreplaceable joy of holding and reading a printed book. Therefore, book fairs should be organized regularly to encourage reading and keep the love of books alive in society.

text: 18. My visit to the book fair A book fair is the exhibition of different kinds of books. It is a place where various kinds of books are displayed for both sale and publicity. People from all walks of life specially the literates and intellectuals throng here. The teens visit the book fair to find their favourite books. However, it is considered an occasion of great festivity to the book lovers. It is usually held in winter in an open place. A huge number of book fairs are held across the country. Among all, the Ekushey Boimela is prominent and noteworthy. Last year, I paid a visit to the Ekushey Boimela. When I heard the opening of the fair, I along with some of my class mates talked to our class teacher and expressed our intention to visit this mega event. After consulting the principal, he agreed to accompany us to the Ekushey Boimela. On the very day, we hired an air-conditioned bus and went to visit this grand fair. We started for the fair around 11 o'clock. We were stunned to watch this great event. A festive look prevailed all around. There was eye catching collections of books everywhere. Security arrangements were very tight and strict. We had to pass through security gate after having being checked by the metal detector. There were so many decorated stalls organized by the publishers. The stalls dealing in stationery and children's books were very impressing. A huge crowd thronged over them. Each publisher had his own way to attract the public either by offering discount or by gift scheme. Some publishers had made special sitting arrangement. We saw some writers busy with their fans to put autographs. I bought a book of my own choice- a book of Shakespeare. But, alas! How time flies on. It was almost 5 o'clock. Our teacher told us to pack up and we all approached towards the gate to avail of the return journey. Really it was an awesome and memorable trip for us.

text: 19. Price Hike Price hike is the sudden increase in prices or costs of commodities. Price hike is a serious bane on the economy of Bangladesh. The vast majority of the consumers are hard hit by the recent hike in prices of the daily necessities. Price hike is caused by short supply of commodities and inflation. The price hike that results from short supply of commodities is temporary. The prices come down if their supply is enhanced. But the price hike caused by inflation cannot be controlled by easy efforts. Market syndicate is mostly responsible for price hike in Bangladesh. Moreover, some dishonest traders create artificial crisis by storing essential commodities with a view to making huge profit. Whatever may be the cause, price hike causes a lot of hardships and sufferings to people. The lower income groups are severely affected by it because they have to maintain their families with their low and fixed income. It is very difficult for them to meet the excessive prices. Usually, they have to buy less or inferior quality items. They find it difficult to make their both ends meet. They have to go without many things that are necessary to lead a normal life. The working class are the worst sufferers. Price hike leads to the widespread corruption and moral degradation. If price hike continues at this prevailing degree, the people of our country may face starvation  at a large scale. Price hike is a problem which cannot be solved overnight. All syndicates of the businessmen have to be suppressed boldly and inflation of money should be controlled. Annual budget in agricultural sector has to be increased to increase agri-products. Public awareness should be raised in this respect.

text: 20. A Street Hawker A person who sells various things moving from street to street, is called a street hawker. A street hawker is a familiar figure in towns. He usually lives in a hut or in a slum. He is the vendor of a variety of things. He carries his things sometimes on the head, sometimes in his hands and at some other times in a small hand-cart. He sells toys, bangles, ribbons, clothings, sweets, fruits, utensils, newspapers, fancy goods and things of domestic use by bringing them up to people's doors. He is a clever fellow who knows how to run his business. He brings one kind of goods for children, another kind for womenfolk and yet another kind for men. He chooses things for children that will attract children and for women that will attract women. So, he has to be very careful about what things to sell. Thus he earns his living by selling different things to his customers. As he passes along the street, he makes a peculiar cry to attract the attention of his customers. The children here and there gather around him and stop him. The hawker generally chooses a time when the master of the family is away and the women are free from their household duties. He hopes thereby to make a good bargain. In towns everywhere we see hawkers and many of them are making good business. There is another kind of hawkers who buy newspapers, blank pots, old books, worn-out clothes from the houses. However, children are fond of a hawker but men do not like them. By carrying the goods for sale in his hands or on his shoulders, he moves from door to door. Sometimes, he goes from one street to another with his moving shop. A street hawker's life is not easy and comfortable but it is rather difficult and full of hardship.

text: 21. Water Pollution Water pollution means contamination of surface and drinking water to the extent that they are harmful to use. Pure water is essential for health. We cannot live a single day without water. It is the main element of our body. So, the another name of water is life. People pollute water in a variety of ways. Chemical fertilizers and insecticides are used in the fields by the farmers. Rain and floods wash away some of these chemicals and mix them with the water of rivers, canals and ponds. Besides these, mills and factories throw their waste into rivers and canals and pollute water. Oil, food and human waste from boats, launches and steamers get mixed with the water of rivers and canals. A lot of latrines are built on the banks of rivers and canals. A number of kutcha drains run into the rivers and canals. These drains carry human waste and filth and let them fall into water. Thus, it is clear that ignorant people are responsible for water pollution. Polluted water is in no way safe. Water filled with filth, dust and germs is in every way harmful to human life. People get attacked with various waterborne diseases after drinking polluted water. To live a healthy life, we should take all-out measures to minimize water pollution. The first and foremost measure to minimize water pollution is proper management of all sorts of waste. Farmers have to be made aware of using chemical fertilizers and insecticides. Above all, general people should be made aware  of the negative effects of polluted water so that they refrain from polluting water.

text: 22. A Village Fair You Experienced A village fair is a common affair in our country. It is generally held every year at a fixed time. It is an annual event. Many people gather in a village fair to buy and sell things. Moreover, it is a great source of recreation to them. A village fair is usually held in an open space to which communication is easy. People from many places come to a village fair to buy as well as to sell their goods. A village fair is a gay affair. I have been to such a fair recently. I was accompanied by two of my bosom friends. There were different types of stalls in different rows, such as: book-stalls, stalls for dolls and toys, electronic goods, hand-made goods, etc. All stalls were almost crowded. We walked round the fair and enjoyed it very much. My friends and I rode on merry-go-round. I bought some toys for my younger sister. A village fair is a place of fun and enjoyment for the children living around. It dispels the monotony of the village people. All categories of people- poor and rich, children and adults, men and women, illiterate and educated çame crowding in the village fair and enjoyed the best of it according to their taste and temperament. The most delighted were the children. They bought cheap flutes, toys, sweets and fancy goods, rode the merry-go-round and feel terribly  happy and excited. A village fair bears a great significance to the people belonging to all walks of life. Villagers take it as a source of their recreation. It helps them to remove dullness from their mind. Moreover, it plays a vital role to the rural economy also. The experience I have got from visiting the fair will be ever fresh in my mind.

text: 23. Mobile Phone A mobile phone is one of the greatest inventions of modern science and it has become an essential part of our daily life. It has made communication very easy and quick. In the past, people had to depend on letters or landline telephones to contact others, but now we can talk to anyone at any time from anywhere in the world within a few seconds. A mobile phone is small, light, and easy to carry, so we can keep it in our pocket and use it whenever necessary. It is not only used for making calls but also for sending text messages, taking photos, recording videos, browsing the internet, and using different social media platforms. Students can attend online classes, search for information, and prepare their lessons with the help of a mobile phone. Business people can communicate with clients, send emails, and join online meetings easily. In emergency situations, a mobile phone is very helpful because we can quickly call the police, a doctor, or family members. It also has GPS facilities which help us find unknown places. However, excessive use of mobile phones can be harmful. It may waste valuable time, reduce attention in studies, and cause health problems such as eye strain and headache. Therefore, we should use mobile phones properly and avoid becoming addicted to them. In conclusion, a mobile phone is a wonderful gift of science that makes our life easier and more comfortable when used wisely.

text: 24. A Day Labourer A day labourer is a person who does heavy and unskilled manual work. He is usually employed in agricultural or construction work. So, he needs more strength than skill. He works hard from morning to evening, but he is ill-paid at the end of the day. He cannot meet his bare necessities of life with his scanty income. A day labourer usually lives in a slum and leads a very miserable life with his family members. Though he works hard and works hard and engages himself for the whole day long, he he cannot earn more than his needs. Whenever he car can earn a little bit more, his joy knows no bounds. He buys delicious food and enjoys a good meal with his family. During harvest, he is in great demand and he earns more. But when there is less or no work, his sorrows know no bounds. Sometimes, he has to starve with his family.. Actually, in respect of work, a day labourer has no choice. Sometimes, he is seen coming out of his house with a a spade and a basket. text: 28. A School Magazine A school magazine is a literary magazine to foster the latent talent of the students, educators and staff of a school. It is also called the masterpiece of a school. Generally a school magazine is published once a year. It usually begins with the foreword of the Headmaster/ the Principal and followed by the Annual Report. The report includes the outstanding result and the excellent performance of a school. It reveals the creative genius and inner faculties of the stakeholders of a school. It is a blend of stories, essays, plays, poetry, puzzles, quizzes, riddles, jokes, comics or any other kind of literary text. The publication of a school magazine committee is not so an easy task. So, in order to conduct the work of the publication of a school magazine, the form of a school magazine committee is a must. The Headmaster/ The Principal is the chief patron of the committee. The committee generally consists nine members. Three of them are teachers, and six of them are students. The Headmaster/ The principal selects the editor from the teachers. And the editor invites the writings from the students, teachers and staffs. After scrutinizing the writings, the best type of writings are selected for printing. But, before going to print, the proof readers have to check the selected writings to avoid mistakes or make them flawless. In fact, a school magazine helps both the students and teachers to develop their power of thinking and writing skills. Hence, it is considered as a key stone to become great writers and poets in the future. In a word, a school magazine reflects the academic, cultural and co-curricular activities as a literary mirror of a school.

text: 29. Air Pollution Air pollution is a major environmental issue that poses a serious threat to life on Earth. It refers to the presence of harmful and unwanted substances in the atmosphere that contaminate the air we breathe. These pollutants mainly include smoke, carbon monoxide, sulfur dioxide, nitrogen oxides, dust particles, and toxic chemicals. The primary sources of air pollution are industrial factories, motor vehicles, brick kilns, power plants, and the burning of fossil fuels like coal and oil. Rapid urbanization and deforestation also contribute significantly to this problem. Air pollution has both immediate and long-term effects on human health. It can cause respiratory diseases such as asthma, bronchitis, lung infections, and even heart problems. Children and the elderly are especially vulnerable. In addition, polluted air harms animals, damages crops, and destroys ecosystems. It also contributes to global warming, the greenhouse effect, acid rain, and ozone layer depletion, all of which have disastrous impacts on the planet. To combat air pollution, it is essential to adopt cleaner technologies, use public transport, enforce environmental regulations, and raise public awareness. Tree plantation and the use of renewable energy sources like solar and wind power are also effective ways to improve air quality. If we fail to address this growing problem, it may lead to irreversible damage to our environment and threaten the future of all living beings.

text: 31. A Bus Station A bus station is a place where buses arrive, depart, and halt to carry passengers from one place to another. It is usually located in a busy part of a city or town to make transportation convenient for people. A typical bus station has ticket counters, waiting rooms, sheds for shade, and sometimes small shops, tea stalls, and food corners for the convenience of passengers. From early morning till late at night, the station remains crowded with travelers, conductors, drivers, and hawkers. Buses of various routes stand in rows, and conductors call out the names of their destinations in loud voices. Passengers can be seen buying tickets, waiting with luggage, or rushing to catch their buses. The continuous arrival and departure of buses, the honking of horns, and the busy movement of people create a lively but often noisy atmosphere. Porters help carry heavy bags, while hawkers sell snacks, water, and newspapers. Although sometimes overcrowded and chaotic, a bus station is a vital part of our communication system, helping people travel easily, affordably, and quickly from one place to another.

text: 32. A Train Station A train station is an important place where trains stop to pick up and drop off passengers. It is usually full of life, movement, and activity from early morning until late at night. As one enters a train station, the first thing noticed is the ticket counter where passengers buy tickets for their journey. On the platform, passengers wait eagerly for their trains, while loudspeakers announce the arrival and departure times. Vendors move through the crowd selling tea, coffee, biscuits, and snacks, creating a lively atmosphere. Coolies dressed in red shirts carry heavy luggage for passengers. Station staff work hard to ensure that everything runs on time and safely. The sight of a train approaching, with its loud whistle and puffing engine, fills the place with excitement. A train station is not only a place of travel but also a meeting point of people from different walks of life, making it an important hub for communication and connection between cities and villages.

text: 33. Environment Pollution Environment pollution is one of the most alarming problems in the modern world. It refers to the contamination of our surroundings by harmful substances that disrupt the balance of nature and threaten the survival of living beings. The major forms of pollution include air pollution, water pollution, soil pollution, noise pollution, and plastic pollution. Air pollution is mainly caused by the smoke emitted from vehicles, factories, and burning of fossil fuels, which increases greenhouse gases and leads to global warming. Water pollution occurs when industrial waste, chemicals, plastics, and untreated sewage are discharged into rivers, seas, and ponds, making water unsafe for humans, animals, and aquatic plants. Soil pollution results from excessive use of chemical fertilizers, pesticides, and dumping of non-biodegradable materials, which reduce soil fertility and damage crops. Noise pollution, created by vehicles, loudspeakers, and industrial activities, harms human health by causing stress, hearing problems, and lack of concentration. Plastic pollution has become a global crisis because plastic is non-degradable and accumulates in land and water, endangering marine life and human health. The consequences of pollution are extremely dangerous, including climate change, rising temperatures, melting of polar ice, destruction of biodiversity, spread of diseases, and scarcity of pure food and water. To combat this issue, governments, organizations, and individuals must work together. Strict laws should be enforced against industries that pollute, and people should be encouraged to plant more trees, use eco-friendly products, recycle waste, and reduce the use of plastics. Public awareness is essential to inspire everyone to protect the environment. If we fail to act in time, our planet will face irreversible damage, making life unbearable for future generations. Therefore, it is our duty to keep the environment clean and safe for all living beings.

text: 34. A bus stand A bus stand is a place where buses stop to pick up and drop off passengers, Usually it is located in busy areas such as marketplaces, near schools, offices, or important roads. Cleanliness is another challenge, as many bus stands become dirty due to litter and waste, creating an unpleasant environment. Pickpockets may also take advantage of the crowd, making it necessary for passengers to remain alert. Despite these problems, a bus stand is an essential part of urban and rural life because it helps people travel quickly and affordably from one place to another. It is not just a point of departure or arrival, but a place where people from different walks of life come together, interact briefly, and then continue on their separate journeys.

text: 35. A Book Fair You Visited / Visiting A Book Fair Every year in February, I visit the Ekushe Book Fair, which is officially known as Amar Ekushey Book Fair. Like the previous years, I have visited the Ekushey Boi Mela. The fair is organized by the Bangla Academy to commemorate the historic Language Movement. The fair is held in the premises of Bangla Academy in Dhaka and attracts thousands of book lovers from all over the country. When I entered the fair, I saw beautifully decorated stalls filled with new books of different genres such as novels, poems, essays, research works, and children’s literature. Famous writers come to the fair to meet readers and sign autographs, which creates great excitement among visitors. The environment of the fair is very lively and inspiring. People of all ages, especially students and young readers, gather there with great enthusiasm. I bought several books written by my favourite authors and also enjoyed cultural programs arranged in the evening. The fair reminds us of the sacrifices made by the martyrs of the Language Movement in 1952. It not only promotes our literature and culture but also encourages people to develop the habit of reading books. Visiting the Ekushe Book Fair was a memorable and enriching experience for me.

text: 11. Science in everyday life A journey you  have enjoyed / A memorable event. A railway journey is always an interesting and pleasant experience in life. I have travelled by train many times on different occasions. But some journeys remain vivid in life and cannot be vanished from memory. The journey that I made during the last summer vacation was one of the memorable events in my life. I feel excited to describe it. The news while visiting my uncle's house in Chittagong pleased me heavily. My uncle is a government employee who has been living there since last five years. My parents decided to visit the place by train and it filled my heart with joy. The date was fixed on the day of first holiday. My father bought five tickets of the Intercity Express. Our Journey started from the kamlapur Rail station. The station was very nice. There was great noise all around. We reached the station just before the train left. As the train arrived, the passengers were rushing towards the compartments. I observed some passengers were sitting on the roof. They all were in a cheerful mind. We all were ready. When first bell rang before the starting of the train all the passengers seemed to remain alert so that nothing was left behind. Finally, the train left the Kamalapur station. We heaved a sigh of relief after starting the train. The train halted at every station. It ran between cities, bazars, green fields, valleys hills and forests. The train was always in high speed. The distant trees seemed to move in the opposite direction. But the sights that attracted me most on the way were that of the green fields, valleys, hills and the even green forest of Bangladesh. Besides the red glow of the sun and the flying birds all around provided the charming scene to me. I could have a good look outside. I enjoyed myself with scene the farmers  were working in the field. The green fields and the yellow-colored paddy fields, the valley of the hills and the trees,and  the forests fascinated me profusely. I felt thrilled every moment. There were friendly passengers all around and I talked with some of them. Luckily, I got a good seat near window. One special thing impressed me very much was that the people in the train waved their hands to say good bye to their relatives and friends on the platform at every station that came to see them off. As I was on the side of a window, I could enjoy a cool breeze and see the natural scenery outside. But the people inside the train were busy reading news papers and magazines or talking with others on the current political and social events of the country. After about ten hours of Journey, the train came to a halt at Chittagong station. We had at last reached our destination. We got down and walked a short distance and reached my uncle's house. In conclusion, I may say that the eventful journey was a very pleasant one. I can never forget this journey. The reminiscences of this journey often flicker in my mind every now and then. It was, by far, the most unforgettable journey of my life that has left its deep impression on the canvas of my life and this is the most  memorable event in my life. Population Problem  of Bangladesh Population problem is one of the most talked about topics of the world now a days. In Bangladesh, it has been declared as number one national problem. Hence Bangladesh is called an over populated country. In fact, her present population has come to a stage of explosion. The population of Bangladesh has become a great problem. The present growth rate of population is 1.147 percent. rcent. This rate is very alarming and disturbing. If this growth rate continues, her population will be double by the end of 25th century. Now about 16 core people live in Bangladesh, a country of 147570 square km making Bangladesh one of the ten most populous countries in the world. The population of Bangladesh is increasing by geometrical progressing while agricultural production is increasing by arithmetical progression. The population becomes problem if the overall density of population is much higher than its territory, property and ability to feed them. If we think about it in deeper level, we can find many causes of population problem. These causes are discussed in detail below. Illiteracy is the most important cause of population problem in Bangladesh. The illiterate people are not aware of their responsibilities and they cannot realize the importance of family planning. So they become parents of so many poor children. Religious bigotry is another cause of over population in the country. Some orthodox people opine that to adopt family planning measure is a sinful act. As a result, population goes on increasing day by day. In Bangladesh death rate is lower than birth rate that contributes to increase new faces every year. The system of polygamy has worsened the population problem in the country. The custom of early marriage is still prevalent in the rural areas. It is the prime cause of increasing population at an alarming rate. 50% of the total population of our country are of the female sex. Most of them are illiterate. They are not aware of their next generation. They don't follow family planning. So they give birth to many children every year. Over population creates a lot of problems. In addition, this problem acts as a national problem of the country. We see several adverse impacts on the society for over population. A poor country like Bangladesh, having a huge population, cannot provide the proper midicare. Over population creates unemployment problem. An increase in the population has a direct impact on the unrest and various forms of crimes. An increase in population hampers education, medical facilities and other basic needs of people as government is not able to ease these facilities for everyone. The problem of overpopulation should be properly addressed. So the following measures should be taken to control the problem- To educate the masses. To adopt family planning. To award parents having only two children. Creating awareness among the unconscious people about the adverse effect of over population. Stopping early marriage. Facilitate female education and erecting employment opportunities. In conclusion,it can be said that the growth rate of Bangladesh is alarming. The impact of over population is so grievous and pathetic that there is no way other than solving the problem. On the other hand, we should find out the ways and means to utilize the existing population so that they can be manpower instead of being problem for the country. Science in everyday life Science plays a vital role in our daily lives. It is all around us and has become an inseparable part of modern human life. It has made our lives easier, faster, and more comfortable. From the moment we wake up to the time we go to sleep, we are surrounded by the wonders of science. One of the most important contributions of science is in the field of communication. Mobile phones, the internet, and television are all gifts of science. They help us stay connected with the world and access information within seconds. For example, we can talk to someone far away through a video call or send a message instantly. Social media, email, and online news are all possible due to scientific inventions. Similarly, science has made our household tasks much easier. Appliances like refrigerators, washing machines, microwave ovens, vacuum cleaners, and electric irons save both time and effort. These machines help us complete chores quickly and comfortably, which gives us more time for rest and relaxation. In the field of transportation, science has brought revolutionary changes. In the past, people had to travel long distances on foot or by animal carts, which took days or even weeks. But today, we have cars, buses, trains, and airplanes that can carry us to faraway places in just a few hours. These inventions save time and make travel more convenient. Science has also made great progress in the field of medicine. Many diseases that were once deadly can now be cured, thanks to modern treatments and vaccines. Hospitals use scientific machines like X-ray, ECG, and MRI scanners to detect diseases early and treat patients properly. Because of medical science, people are now living longer and healthier lives. Moreover, science helps in agriculture too. Farmers use modern tools, chemical fertilizers, and scientific methods to grow more food in less time. Machines like tractors and harvesters have replaced hard manual labor. As a result, we have more food to feed the growing population. Science also plays a big role in education. Students now use computers, tablets, and projectors in classrooms. Online learning has made it possible to attend classes from anywhere in the world. Science has made education more interactive and enjoyable. Even in entertainment, science has had a strong impact. We can watch movies, listen to music, play video games, or browse the internet at any time. All these forms of entertainment are made possible through modern technology. In conclusion, science is a blessing in our daily lives. It has touched every aspect of human life and continues to improve it every day. Without science, our lives would be slow, hard, and uncomfortable. However, we must also be careful to use science wisely and responsibly. If used properly, science can continue to make the world a better place for everyone.

I have travelled by train many times on different occasions. But some journeys remain vivid in life and cannot be vanished from memory. The journey that I made during the last summer vacation was one of the memorable events in my life. I feel excited to describe it. The news while visiting my uncle's house in Chittagong pleased me heavily. My uncle is a government employee who has been living there since last five years. My parents decided to visit the place by train and it filled my heart with joy. The date was fixed on the day of first holiday. My father bought five tickets of the Intercity Express. Our Journey started from the kamlapur Rail station. The station was very nice. There was great noise all around. We reached the station just before the train left. As the train arrived, the passengers were rushing towards the compartments. I observed some passengers were sitting on the roof. They all were in a cheerful mind. We all were ready. When first bell rang before the starting of the train all the passengers seemed to remain alert so that nothing was left behind. Finally, the train left the Kamalapur station. We heaved a sigh of relief after starting the train. The train halted at every station. It ran between cities, bazars, green fields, valleys hills and forests. The train was always in high speed. The distant trees seemed to move in the opposite direction. But the sights that attracted me most on the way were that of the green fields, valleys, hills and the even green forest of Bangladesh. Besides the red glow of the sun and the flying birds all around provided the charming scene to me. I could have a good look outside. I enjoyed myself with scene the farmers  were working in the field. The green fields and the yellow-colored paddy fields, the valley of the hills and the trees,and  the forests fascinated me profusely. I felt thrilled every moment. There were friendly passengers all around and I talked with some of them. Luckily, I got a good seat near window. One special thing impressed me very much was that the people in the train waved their hands to say good bye to their relatives and friends on the platform at every station that came to see them off. As I was on the side of a window, I could enjoy a cool breeze and see the natural scenery outside. But the people inside the train were busy reading news papers and magazines or talking with others on the current political and social events of the country. After about ten hours of Journey, the train came to a halt at Chittagong station. We had at last reached our destination. We got down and walked a short distance and reached my uncle's house. In conclusion, I may say that the eventful journey was a very pleasant one. I can never forget this journey. The reminiscences of this journey often flicker in my mind every now and then. Trios expose not only beauty but also put immense benefit for human being and other living being in the world. So planting of trees has been given priority to increase the beauty of nature and put the environment in a better position.. Since the dawn of civilization, there were innumerable trees all over the world. But with the passage of time, men started cutting of trees indiscriminately exposing a threat to our environment. Now it is a global concern. People all over world are eager to plant more trees and like go with the campaign. "Grow more trees and save the environment". With the absence of required and sufficient trees, the world's temperature is getting warmer gradually., This situation has changed our climate and eco-system tremendously and caused green house effect. Only plantation of more trees can prevent the environment from pollution. Without oxygen we cannot live for a moment that we get from the tree. Trees take in carbon dioxide and produce oxygen. Carbon dioxide makes our atmosphere polluted. Trees consume this cardon dioxide and thereby makes our environment safe for us. Other than the aforesaid issues, trees came to our life in many ways. We get fruits, flowers, fire woods and timbers. Fruit bearing trees provide us with many types of fruits for our good health. They give us shade and reduce our tiredness. They are used as timber for making ey-catching furniture. Tree plantation draws attention of the people to the fact of environmental degradation all over the world. Due to lack of required forest land, the world experiences less rainfall, draught resulting desertification. The low-lying countries of the world like Bangladesh, Maldives, Sreelanka, have to fight against natural disasters like sidre, Ila, Nargis and other cyclones and floods. Thanks to atmospheric pollution, we should plant trees near our houses, villages and in the forest land for forestation with a view to creating new forest. We should also plant trees on the both sides of the roads. We should advise all to plant two trees, if they cut a sigle one. Generally, rainy season is the best time for tree plantation. Most of the tree plants thrive well in rainy season. But only planting the tree is not enough. Because, planted trees may be destroyed by animals or otherwise. So we should be very careful about the newly planted trees. In conclusion, We may say that the government and the private organizations. Should raise awareness of the people for tree plantation to address all the issues related to our existence. Besides, attempts should be made and environmental laws should be implemented and new law should be enacted to stop cutting trees so that we may get a polluted free environment and live in a better world. It is performed for various reasons including strengthening muscles and the cardiovascular systems, honing athletic skills, weight loss or maintenance, as well as the purpose of enjoyment. There are many kinds of physical exercises . They are of two classes, such as body building exercise and outdoor games. A body building exercise can be done inside a room. After all, for doing various kinds of physical exercise, the blood circulation works well and thus we remain free from any disease. Physical exercise is an abiding source of pleasure. Our  mind and soul remain dull, if we don't follow any kind of exercise. It refreshes our mind and keeps our temperament cool and fresh. Regular physical exercise inspires us to do our duties with a fresh mood. We may draw a conclusion saying that physical exercise is good for health and an abiding source of pleasure. It helps to keep our body fit and fresh and prevents heart attack, diabetes, obesity and many other diseases. It also improves our mental strength and removes depression. Population Problem  of Bangladesh Population problem is one of the most talked about topics of the world now a days. In Bangladesh, it has been declared as number one national problem. Hence Bangladesh is called an over populated country. In fact, her present population has come to a stage of explosion. The population of Bangladesh has become a great problem. The present growth rate of population is 1.147 percent. rcent. This rate is very alarming and disturbing. If this growth rate continues, her population will be double by the end of 25th century. Now about 16 core people live in Bangladesh, a country of 147570 square km making Bangladesh one of the ten most populous countries in the world. The population of Bangladesh is increasing by geometrical progressing while agricultural production is increasing by arithmetical progression. The population becomes problem if the overall density of population is much higher than its territory, property and ability to feed them. If we think about it in deeper level, we can find many causes of population problem. These causes are discussed in detail below. Illiteracy is the most important cause of population problem in Bangladesh. The illiterate people are not aware of their responsibilities and they cannot realize the importance of family planning. So they become parents of so many poor children. Religious bigotry is another cause of over population in the country. Some orthodox people opine that to adopt family planning measure is a sinful act. As a result, population goes on increasing day by day. In Bangladesh death rate is lower than birth rate that contributes to increase new faces every year. The system of polygamy has worsened the population problem in the country. The custom of early marriage is still prevalent in the rural areas. It is the prime cause of increasing population at an alarming rate. 50% of the total population of our country are of the female sex. Most of them are illiterate. They are not aware of their next generation. They don't follow family planning. So they give birth to many children every year. Over population creates a lot of problems. In addition, this problem acts as a national problem of the country. We see several adverse impacts on the society for over population. A poor country like Bangladesh, having a huge population, cannot provide the proper midicare. Over population creates unemployment problem. An increase in the population has a direct impact on the unrest and various forms of crimes. An increase in population hampers education, medical facilities and other basic needs of people as government is not able to ease these facilities for everyone. The problem of overpopulation should be properly addressed. So the following measures should be taken to control the problem- To educate the masses. To adopt family planning. To award parents having only two children. Creating awareness among the unconscious people about the adverse effect of over population. Stopping early marriage. Facilitate female education and erecting employment opportunities. In conclusion,it can be said that the growth rate of Bangladesh is alarming. The impact of over population is so grievous and pathetic that there is no way other than solving the problem. On the other hand, we should find out the ways and means to utilize the existing population so that they can be manpower instead of being problem for the country. Science in everyday life Science plays a vital role in our daily lives. It is all around us and has become an inseparable part of modern human life. It has made our lives easier, faster, and more comfortable. From the moment we wake up to the time we go to sleep, we are surrounded by the wonders of science. One of the most important contributions of science is in the field of communication. Mobile phones, the internet, and television are all gifts of science. They help us stay connected with the world and access information within seconds. For example, we can talk to someone far away through a video call or send a message instantly. Social media, email, and online news are all possible due to scientific inventions. Similarly, science has made our household tasks much easier. Appliances like refrigerators, washing machines, microwave ovens, vacuum cleaners, and electric irons save both time and effort. These machines help us complete chores quickly and comfortably, which gives us more time for rest and relaxation. In the field of transportation, science has brought revolutionary changes. In the past, people had to travel long distances on foot or by animal carts, which took days or even weeks. But today, we have cars, buses, trains, and airplanes that can carry us to faraway places in just a few hours. These inventions save time and make travel more convenient. Science has also made great progress in the field of medicine. Many diseases that were once deadly can now be cured, thanks to modern treatments and vaccines. Hospitals use scientific machines like X-ray, ECG, and MRI scanners to detect diseases early and treat patients properly. Because of medical science, people are now living longer and healthier lives. Moreover, science helps in agriculture too. Farmers use modern tools, chemical fertilizers, and scientific methods to grow more food in less time. Machines like tractors and harvesters have replaced hard manual labor. As a result, we have more food to feed the growing population. Science also plays a big role in education. Students now use computers, tablets, and projectors in classrooms. Online learning has made it possible to attend classes from anywhere in the world. Science has made education more interactive and enjoyable. Even in entertainment, science has had a strong impact. We can watch movies, listen to music, play video games, or browse the internet at any time. All these forms of entertainment are made possible through modern technology. In conclusion, science is a blessing in our daily lives. It has touched every aspect of human life and continues to improve it every day. Without science, our lives would be slow, hard, and uncomfortable. However, we must also be careful to use science wisely and responsibly. If used properly, science can continue to make the world a better place for everyone.
